assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue where 'the admission webhook configuration becomes incompatible with the new Kubernetes version'

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Response includes commands 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager' and 'kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager'

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Response provides Option 1 to 'Temporarily Disable the Webhook' as the fastest solution

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Response includes 'kubectl delete validatingwebhookconfigurations cert-manager-webhook' and 'kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfigurations cert-manager-webhook' commands

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Response explicitly calls it 'Temporarily delete' and describes it as an immediate fix followed by permanent fix section, indicating it's temporary

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.30
assertions.reason: Response includes section 'Check your cert-manager version and upgrade to one compatible with Kubernetes 1.30' with command to check current version

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Response has 'Permanent Fix: Upgrade Cert-Manager' section with kubectl apply command to upgrade to v1.15.3

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Response provides Option 2 with kubectl patch commands to set failurePolicy to 'Ignore' as a 'Safer' alternative

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Response includes verification commands like 'kubectl run test-pod' and 'kubectl get pods -A | grep -E "Pending|ContainerCreating"'
